text: Epoxides are essential intermediates in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and epoxy resins, yet achieving their efficient production through sustainable methods remains a challenge. In this study, we report a highly efficient photoelectrochemical (PEC) epoxidation system utilizing a Br⁻/BrO⁻ redox mediator on an α-Fe₂O₃ (hematite) photoanode. This system achieves exceptional performance for a diverse range of alkenes, reaching selectivities greater than 99% and faradaic efficiencies up to 82 ± 4%—metrics that significantly outperform previously reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical benchmarks. Using water as the sole oxygen source, we demonstrate that the reaction proceeds via a non-radical oxygen atom transfer (OAT) pathway. Specifically, Br⁻ is oxidized to BrO⁻ through an OAT process at the hematite surface, and the resulting BrO⁻ species subsequently transfers its oxygen atom to the alkene substrate. The combination of favorable thermodynamics and the non-radical nature of this mediated mechanism ensures high catalytic efficiency and selectivity. This Br⁻/BrO⁻-mediated strategy offers a robust and sustainable approach for the simultaneous production of high-value epoxides and green hydrogen.
